summary: A blistering show dominated by a ferocious Not Fade Away and seamless Black Peter jam, captured on an imperfect FM broadcast that still delivers the excitement.
review: Listeners rave about this 10/6/1977 Tempe performance – the Not Fade Away is described as “insane,” “murderous,” and faster than usual, flowing into a memorable Black Peter sandwich and highlighted by stellar Terrapin Station, Looks Like Rain and Tennessee Jed. The FM broadcast source is a bit rough around the edges (some “crappy soundboard” artifacts and mix variations), but the energy of the band shines through.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive fan commentary on the musical performance (multiple 5‑star rave reviews) outweighs the modest complaints about the broadcast’s technical fidelity.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Off‑air FM broadcast capture; listeners note occasional “crappy” soundboard sections, mix inconsistencies with the full concert (e.g., lyric and solo variations), but overall the recording is listenable and preserves the show’s high energy.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set builds around a lightning‑fast Not Fade Away that segues into an unforgettable Black Peter jam, returns to the groove before moving into Terrapin Station and closing with strong renditions of Looks Like Rain and Tennessee Jed.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers praise Jerry’s “magic” guitar licks, soaring solos and emotive vocals.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s vocal work and the “Bobby W. banshees” solo at the end receive positive nods.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drumming is highlighted as driving the fast Not Fade Away; one reviewer notes the drum mix may differ from other sources.
